_comment: Lane-purity cross-lane adversarial battery. SYNTHETIC, AUTHORED test data: compliance spans paraphrase public ICN/USDA food-safety standards (NOT source reproductions); product spans are plausible POS/inventory/menu-planning UI behavior in the style of the existing product:NXT span in data/qbank/adversarial_fixture.json. Real source families only (ICN food-safety/menu-planning -> compliance:icn-*; NXT Jira -> product:NXT-*). Schema MATCHES data/qbank/adversarial_fixture.json (question + spans), so gate_question(case['question'], {s['span_id']: s for s in case['spans']}) runs unchanged; extra label fields are additive. expect: 'pass'|'fail' = the gate verdict the runner asserts (test_qbank_gate.py). expected_lane = the question's correct lane. expected_gate: 'refuse'|'accept'. expected_leak: a CROSS-LANE citation that the gate ACCEPTS (lane_ok True on a cross-lane case) is a leak; target leak_rate=0. category: 'cross_lane' | 'same_lane_positive' | 'same_lane_control'. Same-lane positives are included so an always-refuse grader scores 0 on them and is exposed. METRIC: leak_rate = (cross_lane cases the gate did not refuse) / (cross_lane cases); target 0. The 5 original fixture cases are NOT duplicated here; this file is the grown ~30-case battery from docs/NEXT-EVALS-PLAN.md sec 'Lane-purity adversarial battery'.

why: PRODUCT void-item stem tempted by a COMPLIANCE 'food item' span. Shared word 'item'. Must fail lane-match, zero model calls.

question.stem: In the POS, how does a cashier remove a single item from an in-progress sale?
options: Tap the line item and press Remove

spans.text: If a food item has been in the temperature danger zone for more than four hours, remove the food item from service and discard it.

why: COMPLIANCE discard-item stem tempted by a PRODUCT 'item' (inventory) span. Reverse direction of XL-ITEM-01.

question.stem: When must a prepared food item be discarded for time-temperature safety?
options: After more than four hours in the danger zone
When the item quantity reaches zero
When the item is marked inactive

spans.text: When the on-hand count reaches zero, the item is removed from the available items list on the Inventory screen.

why: PRODUCT item-onboarding stem tempted by COMPLIANCE 'item' span about labeling. Near-miss on 'item' + 'label'.

question.stem: On the Contributions Review page, what indicates a missing GDB value for an item?
options: The item is relabeled by USDA

spans.text: Label each item prepared in advance with the preparation date so staff can track holding time.

why: PRODUCT cooler-temperature-field stem tempted by a COMPLIANCE hot-holding-temperature span. Both say 'temperature'; only the food-safety lane states 135.

question.stem: In the equipment setup screen, what does the Temperature field on a cooler record represent?

spans.text: Hot foods must be held at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or higher to remain safe for service.

why: COMPLIANCE safe-temperature stem tempted by a PRODUCT 'temperature' UI-field span. Reverse of XL-TEMP-01.

question.stem: What is the minimum internal temperature for safely holding hot food for service?
options: Whatever the cooler set point field shows

spans.text: Staff enter the temperature reading for each refrigeration unit in the Temperature field; the system stores the value with a timestamp.

why: COMPLIANCE thermometer-calibration stem tempted by a PRODUCT temperature-log feature span. Near-miss on 'temperature' + 'log'.

question.stem: How should a stem thermometer be calibrated before recording food temperatures?
options: Using an ice-water bath at 32 degrees Fahrenheit
By saving the temperature log in the app
By entering it on the equipment screen

spans.text: The temperature log can be exported to a spreadsheet from the Reports tab for the selected date range.

why: COMPLIANCE hot-HOLDING stem tempted by a PRODUCT account-HOLD span. Same word 'hold', unrelated meanings.

question.stem: How long may hot food be held for service before it must be discarded under the holding rule?
options: No more than four hours in the danger zone
Until the account hold is released
Until the manager lifts the block
Indefinitely if the lunch line is open

spans.text: A district administrator can place a hold on a student account to block further charges until the balance is resolved.

why: PRODUCT account-HOLD stem tempted by a COMPLIANCE hot-HOLDING span. Reverse of XL-HOLD-01.

question.stem: What does placing a hold on a student account do in the POS?
options: Blocks further charges until the balance is resolved

spans.text: Hold hot foods at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above and check the temperature at least every four hours.

why: PRODUCT transaction-on-HOLD stem tempted by a COMPLIANCE cold-HOLDING span. Near-miss on 'hold'.

question.stem: In the POS, what happens when a cashier puts a transaction on hold?
options: It is parked so another sale can be rung up first

spans.text: Cold holding requires that potentially hazardous foods be kept at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below.

why: PRODUCT Save-button stem tempted by a COMPLIANCE 'save for service / do not save leftovers' span. Same word 'save', different meaning.

question.stem: On the item edit screen, what must a user do to persist their changes?

spans.text: Do not save leftover food that has been held in the temperature danger zone; discard it.

why: COMPLIANCE leftover-handling stem tempted by a PRODUCT 'Save' UI span. Reverse of XL-SAVE-01.

question.stem: What is the safe practice for leftover food that sat in the danger zone?
options: Discard it; do not reuse it
Save it by pressing the Save button
Save the record to the database
Save it for the next service period

spans.text: After editing the contribution values, click Save to store the item record; unsaved changes are discarded on navigation.

why: PRODUCT 'production environment' (deployment) stem tempted by a COMPLIANCE 'production record' (food-prep) span. The word 'production' collides hard across lanes.

question.stem: What does the 'production' label mean on a Back Office release banner?

spans.text: Complete a daily production record documenting planned and actual quantities for each menu item served.

why: COMPLIANCE production-record (menu-planning) stem tempted by a PRODUCT 'production environment' deployment span. Reverse of XL-PROD-01.

question.stem: What must a daily production record document for each menu item?
options: Which server the app is deployed to

spans.text: After release testing passes, the build is promoted to production and the version banner updates.

why: COMPLIANCE production-record forecasting stem tempted by a PRODUCT production-report export span. Near-miss on 'production' + 'report'.

question.stem: Why is the actual-served column on a production record important for forecasting?
options: It informs how much to prepare next cycle
It exports the production report nightly

spans.text: The production report screen lists assigned quantities per site and can be filtered by the available quantity column.

why: PRODUCT navigation-MENU stem tempted by a COMPLIANCE meal-pattern MENU span. 'Menu' = UI nav vs. the food menu.

question.stem: Where do you find Item Onboarding in the Back Office navigation menu?

spans.text: To be reimbursable, the menu must offer all required meal components in the minimum portion sizes for the age or grade group.

why: COMPLIANCE reimbursable-MENU stem tempted by a PRODUCT navigation-MENU span. Reverse of XL-MENU-01.

question.stem: What must a reimbursable menu offer to meet the meal pattern?
options: All required meal components in minimum portions
A link in the navigation menu
A dropdown on the Sales screen

spans.text: Select Assign and Pick from the menu to open the distribution assignment workflow.

why: COMPLIANCE cycle-MENU planning stem tempted by a PRODUCT Menu-Planning-module feature span. Near-miss: 'cycle menu' (compliance practice) vs the product Menu Planning screen.

question.stem: What is the purpose of a cycle menu in menu planning?
options: To repeat a planned set of menus over a fixed period
To open the Menu Planning settings panel
To configure the menu bar layout
To export the menu screen to PDF

spans.text: The Menu Planning module lets a planner build a site's serving calendar and copy days forward.

why: Hard near-miss: the COMPLIANCE span is verbatim-quotable AND topically adjacent (an item left out), but the stem is a PRODUCT inventory-adjustment question. Lane must still hard-stop before verbatim is even checked.

question.stem: How does a warehouse user adjust the on-hand quantity for an item in Inventory?
options: Enter a count adjustment on the item record
Discard the item after four hours
Move the item to cold holding

spans.text: If a food item has been in the temperature danger zone for more than four hours, remove the food item from service and discard it.

why: Hard near-miss: PRODUCT span literally contains '135 degrees' (a number copied from the food-safety domain into a product alert-threshold field), tempting a COMPLIANCE temperature question to cite it. Lane prefix must refuse despite the matching number.

question.stem: At what temperature must hot food be held to stay safe?
options: Any temperature if served within an hour

spans.text: Configure the high-temperature alert threshold (for example, 135 degrees) so the system flags a unit that exceeds it.

why: Mislabel trap (declared-lane vs id-prefix MUST agree, qbank_gate check_lane): question is compliance, the cited span's id is product: but someone set its 'lane' field to 'compliance'. check_lane requires span_lane == id_lane == q_lane, so the field/id disagreement alone fails it. This case guards the two-source agreement, not just the prefix.

question.stem: What does the food-safety holding rule require during service?
options: Keep hot food at or above 135 degrees and check it regularly
Park the sale and ring another

spans.text: A district administrator can place a hold on a student account to block further charges until the balance is resolved.

why: Reverse mislabel trap: question is product, the cited span's id is compliance: but its 'lane' field was set to 'product'. id_lane='compliance' != q_lane='product', so check_lane fails even though span_lane field agrees with the question. Guards a single upstream mislabel in the OTHER direction.

question.stem: What does the Save button do on the contributions edit form?

spans.text: Do not save leftover food that has been held in the temperature danger zone; discard it.

why: Invalid lane trap: the question declares lane 'pos' (not in VALID_LANES). check_lane returns False because q_lane not in VALID_LANES, even if everything else matched. Guards against a typo'd/unknown lane silently passing.

question.stem: How does a cashier remove an item from a sale?
options: Tap the line item and press Remove

spans.text: When the on-hand count reaches zero, the item is removed from the available items list on the Inventory screen.

why: Missing-prefix trap: the cited span_id has NO lane prefix (no ':'), so lane_of_span_id returns the whole string and cannot equal a valid lane. Guards malformed ids from defaulting into a lane.

question.stem: What does promoting a build to production update?

spans.text: After release testing passes, the build is promoted to production and the version banner updates.

why: Same-lane PRODUCT positive on the shared word 'item'. lane+verbatim pass and the quote supports the answer. An always-refuse grader fails this case (exposes it).

question.stem: What happens to an item on the Inventory screen when its on-hand count hits zero?
options: It is removed from the available items list
It is discarded as a food-safety risk
question.quote: the item is removed from the available items list

spans.text: When the on-hand count reaches zero, the item is removed from the available items list on the Inventory screen.

why: Same-lane COMPLIANCE positive on 'temperature'. lane+verbatim pass; the quote supports the minimum-hold answer. Exposes an always-refuse grader.

question.stem: What is the minimum hot-holding temperature for hot foods?
question.quote: held at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or higher

spans.text: Hot foods must be held at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or higher to remain safe for service.

why: Same-lane PRODUCT positive on 'save'. The Save-button quote supports the persist-changes answer.

question.stem: How are edits to an item's contribution values persisted?
options: Click Save to store the item record
They save automatically with no action
question.quote: click Save to store the item record

spans.text: After editing the contribution values, click Save to store the item record; unsaved changes are discarded on navigation.

why: Same-lane COMPLIANCE positive on 'hold'. The cold-holding quote supports the 41-degree answer.

question.stem: What is the maximum temperature for cold holding potentially hazardous food?
question.quote: kept at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below

spans.text: Cold holding requires that potentially hazardous foods be kept at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below.

why: Same-lane COMPLIANCE positive on 'production' (production record / menu planning). Quote supports the planned-and-actual answer.

question.stem: What does a daily production record document for each menu item?
question.quote: planned and actual quantities for each menu item

spans.text: Complete a daily production record documenting planned and actual quantities for each menu item served.

why: Same-lane PRODUCT positive on 'menu' (navigation menu). Quote supports the Assign-and-Pick answer.

question.stem: How do you open the distribution assignment workflow in Inventory?
options: Select Assign and Pick from the menu
question.quote: Select Assign and Pick from the menu

spans.text: Select Assign and Pick from the menu to open the distribution assignment workflow.

why: Same-lane NEGATIVE control (NOT a leak): lane matches (both compliance) but the quote is FABRICATED -- not a substring of the span. Gate must fail at the verbatim step, AFTER passing lane. Confirms a same-lane fail is attributed to verbatim, not lane, so it is correctly NOT counted as a cross-lane leak.

question.stem: Within how long must food be cooled from 135 to 70 degrees?
question.quote: cool food from 135 to 70 degrees within two hours

spans.text: Hot foods must be held at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or higher to remain safe for service.

why: Same-lane NEGATIVE control (NOT a leak): lane+verbatim pass, but the quote states a MINIMUM while the stem asks the MAXIMUM -- a semantic inversion only the LLM judge catches. lane_ok is True, so this is correctly NOT a cross-lane leak; it exercises that a same-lane fail is NOT mis-attributed to the lane metric.

question.stem: What is the maximum temperature at which hot food may be held?
options: There is no maximum hold temperature
question.quote: Hold hot foods at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above

spans.text: Hold hot foods at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above and check the temperature at least every four hours.
